results:
Based on the search results, here's what I found about React error 310:

## What is React Error 310?

In the minified production build of React, full error messages are avoided to reduce bytes sent over the wire. When you encounter this error in production, you need to decode it to understand the actual issue.

## Common Cause: "Rendered more hooks than during the previous render"

The minified error translates to: "Rendered more hooks than during the previous render." This is one of the most common underlying causes of error #310.

## How to Get the Full Error Message

React highly recommends using the development build locally when debugging your app since it tracks additional debug info and provides helpful warnings about potential problems. You can visit React's error decoder at `https://react.dev/errors/310` to get more detailed information.

## Real-World Examples (2025)

Recent reports show this error appearing in various applications:
- Users reported getting this error when logging into the Alpaca markets portal in April 2025
- In Next.js applications, the error "Rendered more hooks than during the previous render" has been reported
- Confluence Database users encountered this error intermittently when interacting with Tag data type columns

## General Troubleshooting

To debug this effectively, switch to your non-minified development environment where you'll see the actual error message, making it much easier to identify and fix the underlying issue in your React code.
